snippet.title: Why You Wouldn't Survive The Bangkok Hilton Prison
snippet.description:
Why You Wouldn't Survive The Bangkok Hilton Prison

Let’s start with the fact that it is a maximum-security prison. It was made by King Rama VII in 1931 for detaining dangerous, long-term, and death row inmates and currently, it is the only establishment in Thailand responsible for the execution of the death penalty by lethal injection. Bang Kwang Central Prison holds death penalty inmates in the death row unit and almost all the prisoners spend much of their time in prison in leg shackles, even when they are behind bars. However, in 2013, the Department of Corrections launched a pilot project to remove the leg shackles from about 560 death row and life sentence inmates to improve its record on prisoner rights. There was a whole ceremony presided over by Prime Minister Yingluck Shinawatra on May 12 2013 to unchain the inmates.

snippet.title: One Degree of Separation | Idaho College and University Presidents | Idaho Reports
snippet.description:
This week, leaders from Idaho’s public four-year institutions and community colleges presented their budget requests to the joint budget committee, focusing on their academic programs and facility needs. Some higher education institutions, particularly Boise State University and the University of Idaho, fielded tough questions from lawmakers this week over diversity and inclusion programs. BSU President Marlene Tromp and UI President Scott Green defended their institutions and challenged some of the numbers lawmakers used to draw their conclusions about the universities’ expenditures. 

On Friday, Melissa Davlin sat down with the presidents of three other higher education institutions: President Kevin Satterlee of Idaho State University, President Gordon Jones of College of Western Idaho, and President Rick Aman of College of Eastern Idaho.

snippet.description:
TV host Anniko van Santen joined the Dutch TV programme 'Beat The Champions' and won the full amount of 16.000 EUR, approximately 584.000 THB. She nominated us to receive all the funds, and gave us the flexibility to use them where we see the highest need.

Many thanks to Anniko's incredible generosity and ongoing support for our cause. Thanks to her, we can now provide even more support to those who need it most.

So let's all join together in a big celebration for Anniko's amazing win! You can watch the thrilling moment when she clinched victory in the video here.
  
#BeatTheChampions #pcfsupporters #donations #sponsors

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Philanthropy Connections Foundation
Based in Chiang Mai, Thailand

Our Motto is to FIND, HELP, AND EMPOWER PEOPLE IN NEED

More information:
Website - https://philanthropyconnections.org/
Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/PhilanthropyConnections

Philanthropy Connections Foundation (PCF) is a non-profit organisation that supports people in vulnerable situations.

We work in partnership with local organisations in Thailand, Cambodia and Burma, who know the real problems and needs within their communities. They speak the language and know the culture, making them the ideal partners to implement projects that will address these problems. What they often lack are the resources to find help from outside.

That is where we can help. We find the required help for them and by doing so enable them to do more for their communities. We connect, we engage, we facilitate and if required, we advise. We shine a light onto small, effective grassroots organisations that otherwise would remain invisible.

snippet.title: Charles and Diana in Australia and Thailand (1988) | Royal Specials
snippet.description:
On 2 February 1988, ITN aired a special programme covering the travels of Prince Charles, Prince of Wales and Diana, Princess of Wales in Australia and Thailand. More specifically, "From Darwin to Bangkok" compiled footage of the Wales' trip to a crocodile farm in Darwin and highlights of the couple's three-day trip to Thailand to celebrate the 60th birthday of Thailand's King Bhumibol Adulyadej. Charles and Diana were hosted by the king and his wife, Queen Sirikit, and spent time with their fellow prince and princesses – including Prince Maha Vajiralongkorn, the crown prince who would in 2016 ascend to the throne. As well as the capital of Bangkok, the Waleses visited the northern city of Chiang Mai as well as the region bordering Burma (officially renamed Myanmar in 1989).

snippet.title: 2023 Asia's 50 Best Awards Ceremony Red Carpet Interview 2023 紅毯採訪亞洲50頒獎典禮
snippet.description:
The Asia's 50 Best Restaurants list was just announced, and this year's results were somewhat surprising yet expected due to the influence of both inherent and external factors such as the voting system and varying degrees of pandemic-related restrictions across different regions. While the list generated a lot of buzz, it should not be used to judge a restaurant's superiority or inferiority. However, the value of the Asia's 50 list in terms of bringing people together cannot be overstated.

This year, Japan and Thailand emerged as the biggest winners, with five of the top ten restaurants located in Japan, four in Bangkok, and one in Singapore. Of the top 50 restaurants, Japan had the most with ten, followed by Thailand and Singapore with nine each, and South Korea and Mainland China with four each. Hong Kong had five restaurants on the list. This outcome is related to the timing of each country's border reopening. Thailand was the first Asian country to fully reopen its borders, and Japan followed suit in October last year, attracting a large number of tourists and boosting fine dining in the region. As a result, these countries had a greater chance of topping the list. On the other hand, China only reopened in January this year, and the restrictions on foreign visitors entering the country have inevitably affected its ranking.

Overall, I am not disappointed with this year's results because the list reflects decisions made ten years ago. In the food and beverage industry today, working hard, having the opportunity to meet and exchange ideas, and meeting more people are just as important as improving one's culinary skills. These ingredients, along with culinary skills, can complement each other and propel rapid growth. The Asia's 50 list is the best platform for this, and its significance goes beyond the ranking numbers.

亞洲 50 最佳餐廳剛剛揭曉，今年在投票分配設計先天因素，加上疫情後各地區開放時間早晚等後天因素影響下，榜單結果雖讓人意外卻也是意料之中，即使話題性很高，但這份名單不能借鑒來評判一家餐廳優劣，然而對於重聚這個價值而言，亞洲 50 的重要性仍是無價。
 
今年榜單中，日本與泰國無疑是最大贏家，前十大餐廳中 5 家位於日本，4 家位於泰國曼谷，一家是新加坡餐廳。前 50 排名中日本 10 家最多，泰國與新加坡各 9 家，韓國與中國大陸餐廳各 4 家入榜，香港則有 5 家入榜。這樣的結果與國境開放時間早晚有關，泰國是亞洲最早全面開放邊境的國家，日本也是去年十月開放國門，政策一出頓時吸引大批遊客前往，取消疫情相關管制對當地 Fine Dining 的提振幫助很大，在排名上更容易搶下頭香，反觀中國自今年一月才開放，外國遊客入境受限下自然影響排名表現。

snippet.title: "Whoever governs Singapore must have that iron in him": Lee Kuan Yew | From the archives
snippet.description:
To mark Lee Kuan Yew's 100th birth anniversary on Sep 16, 2023, CNA is looking back at his more memorable quotes. https://cna.asia/leekuanyewquotes

In one of his most iconic speeches, Mr Lee Kuan Yew spoke to a rapt crowd who gathered in the rain at Fullerton Square for PAP's lunch-time rally during the 1980 General Election. Using the dispute with Singapore Airlines as an example, the then-Prime Minister described in no uncertain terms the qualities needed to run the country and the lengths he would go to ensure Singapore's success. Watch an excerpt from his speech on Dec 19, 1980.

snippet.title: My most viral cancer recovery recipe!
snippet.description:
My most viral cancer recovery recipe! ♥️

If you are looking for a simple, high protein breakfast (that does not include protein powder) - THIS IS IT!

It also makes a great snack or dessert 🍮

Ingredients:
-40 g oats
-1/2 banana
-Pinch of cinnamon
-100 mL water
-100 g Greek yogurt

Directions:
Mix oats, water, 1/2 banana, and cinnamon until combined. Spread yogurt evenly over oat layer. Leave in the fridge overnight (or for 2 hours). Top with banana slices and sprinkle with cinnamon.

Enjoy 😋

snippet.title: Gallery Children’s Biennale | Artist Interview: Ly Yeow
snippet.description:
Ly Yeow is an artist and illustrator from Singapore. In 2011, she taught herself to draw as a way to express her feelings. She experiments every day with new ways of making art, including painting with watercolour and making paper cuts. Ly loves to draw sitting next to a window seat that has light shining through the window, and lit candles are a must! She hopes to be a mermaid in her next life, or a marine creature that lives in the sea!

-
Gallery Children's Biennale 2023: Let's Make A Better Place 

The Gallery Children’s Biennale has been a key programme of National Gallery Singapore since 2017, embodying the Gallery’s commitment to inspiring young, curious minds with art. It aims to explore the many ways that children can learn and play while also engaging more with the world around them.

This year, the Biennale features 11 interactive and immersive artworks responding to the theme of “Let’s Make A Better Place,” inviting children and adults to discover how we can create a world built upon this year’s core values of care, respect, imagination and collaboration.

To learn more, please visit https://childrensbiennale.com

snippet.title: Laureate Recital Series. William Yeh, violin
snippet.description:
Presenting brilliant young violinist William Yeh, in a fabulous live concert and interview - get to know him as both a musician and a person. 
We are moving music performance towards POST-pandemic, not "back" to where it PRE-pandemic! 
Live music is just that - LIVE. Whether performance is in a physical space like a concert hall, or in virtual space. Virtual stage is developing, it's reaching further, it's re-engaging the connection between musicians and people who can't be at this or that physical location, at the time of the concert. 
Get involved with our programs, audition for the opportunities to perform at Carnegie Hall and with orchestras around the world, and you are automatically entered to win our SEASON AWARDS. This kind of presentation is among the Prizes we and our partners provide for musicians. 
Application and Awards links in comments. 
Sound Espressivo 2021 Laureate William Yeh, violin, is presented in a solo Recital. Accompanied by Akiko Chiba, piano

Program:
10:20 - Bartók – Romanian Folk Dances
17:35 - Mozart – Violin Sonata No.23 in A major K.305
27:42 - Kreisler – Syncopation
30:48 - Tchaikovsky – Sérénade mélancolique in B-flat minor, Op.26
41:10 - Sarasate – Zapateado, Op.23
45:05 - Dvořák – Songs My Mother Taught Me, B.104, Op.55
49:58 - Paganini (arr. F. Kreisler) – La Campanella, Op.7

Interview with Dr. Anna Ouspenskaya

William Yeh (12), born in London, currently studies with Professor Naoko Tanaka at Juilliard Pre-College. William is grateful for having received generous/full scholarship awards from various musical institutes, including 2019 HeifetzPEG (Program for Exceptionally Gifted, USA; as the youngest violinist that year) and 2022 Aspen Music Festival And School. William has performed and won top prizes in various international competitions, including first prizes (Junior Division) at 2020 Philadelphia International Music Festival Concerto Competition (USA), 2021 EUROASIA International Violin Competition (Japan), 2022 NOMEA International Violin Competition (Italy), and most recently, 2022 New York Chamber Players Orchestra Concerto Competition (USA). William was also awarded Young Artist Awards at 2020 Singapore Violin Festival (Singapore) and 2021 Busan Maru International Music Festival Concerto Competition (South Korea); and the 2nd Prize (Age Group of 11-15) at 2022 Hong Kong International Young Musicians Competition (Hong Kong). Additionally, William has performed at venues, such as Carnegie Hall (New York), Harris Concert Hall (Colorado), Mellon Hall (Philadelphia), National Concert and Recital Halls (Taiwan), and San Vito al Tagliamento Auditorium (Italy). Most recently, William performed with internationally renowned pianist Akiko Chiba, Jinhee Park, Jeong-Eun Lee, Grammy Award-nominated pianist Mark Livshits, and internationally renowned violinist Svetlana Makarova and Accademia d’Archi Arrigoni under the baton of Maestro Giancarlo Guarino. William will perform as a violin soloist in New York with the NYCP Orchestra for the season of 2022-23.

Prior to Juilliard Pre-College, William studied with Professor Chengtu Su (Taipei National University of the Arts, Taiwan), Professor Qian Zhou (Yong Siew Toh Conservatory of Music, Singapore), and violinist Sirena Huang (USA). William performed at the masterclasses with Italian-German-American Grammy-winning violinist Augustin Hadelich (USA), Professor Itzhak Rashkovsky (Ukraine/Israel), Professor Kyung-Sun Lee (South Korea), and the Grammy Award-winning Parker Quartet (USA). Also, William has been mentored by violinist Pieter Schoeman (Concertmaster of the London Philharmonic Orchestra, UK).

Besides playing the violin, William enjoys playing chess, badminton, and ice-skating, as well as singing and dancing in musicals. He played “Rolf” in “The Sound of Music” at the Auditorium of CKS (Chiang Kai-Shek) Memorial Hall and starred as “Peter Pan” in “Peter Pan” at USC (Shih-Chien University) Concert Hall. William has also been awarded the Ouspensky Fund “Conducting” Award and is receiving conducting apprenticeship from Maestro Giacomo Franci, the Artistic Director and the Conductor of The New York Chamber Players Orchestra.
